violations.text: Menu is lacking an allergen statement. Owner/operator is to send picture of updated menu to inspector. lori.marchand@health.ri.gov New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Paper products are to be used for service. 3-bay sink must be used to wash, rinse, and sanitize until dishwasher is serviced and reaching proper sanitizing concentration. Owner/operator is to send proof (positive test strip) of proper concentration to inspector. lori.marchand@health.ri.gov New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Foods are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. Owner/operator is to send picture of updated menu to inspector. lori.marchand@health.ri.gov New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Thermometers are lacking in the prep unit. All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer inside the unit that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026
Menu is lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. The hand-washing sink at the bar was lacking soap and paper towels. Hand-sinks must always be supplied with soap and paper towels for proper hand-washing. Cleaning supplies observed in the bar hand-washing sink. Hand-washing sink must not be used for anything but hand-washing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Raw animal foods (shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables, condiments) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw bacon observed in the same container as raw chicken. Raw animal foods must be separated (below) from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Types of raw animal foods must be stored to prevent cross contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine is being emptied to be properly cleaned and sanitized. Bagged ice is to be used until ice machine has been cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Paper products are to be used, or 3-bay sink to wash, rinse, and sanitize until dishwasher is serviced and reaching proper concentration of sanitizer. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Ambient temperature of the walk-in cooler was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Food products observed with temperatures of 43 - 44 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Service call placed. TCS foods moved to properly functioning refrigeration unit. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Foods are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2026

Thermometers are lacking in the prep unit and condiment slide cold refrigeration unit(s). All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer inside the unit that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 01/26/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026
Menu is lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine is being emptied to be cleaned and sanitized. Bagged ice is to be used until ice machine is cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Ambient temperature of the prep unit was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food products in this unit observed with temperatures of 43 degrees F. Foods were moved to the walk-in cooler. No TCS foods are to be stored in this unit until it is maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Thermometers are lacking in refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the bar dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at the bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. 3-bay sink must be used to sanitize until dishwasher is serviced and reaching proper concentration of sanitizer. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

Thermometer is lacking in the refrigerator. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023
Bar handsink is lacking splashguards to prevent contamination. Splashguards must be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
The doors to the restrooms are not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
The floor and wall junctures behind the sinks do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
